title: barleria eburnea
intro: Barleria eburnea, also known as the white thorny exocrine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Barleria eburnea include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Barleria eburnea can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are then consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
